# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Humankind
- Steam AppID of the game: 1124300

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 2070
- Video driver version: nvidia 580.82.09
- Kernel version: Linux 6.16.8-arch3-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [Gist](https://gist.github.com/skulwicki/24aabc5722058a952779cab1e53f9d6d)
- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1124300.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22621042/steam-1124300.log)

## Symptoms
Current Proton Experimental appears to have a regression with Humankind. Launching the game plays an opening pre-rendered video. Letting the video run to completion or clicking to skip past the video see the game crash and unable to reach the main menu. The game had been running perfectly fine prior to the latest Experimental update from 9/19/25. Previous Proton versions 10.0-2(beta) and 9.0-4 are still working for me as of today.


## Reproduction
1. Launch Humankind
2. Either wait for the opening video to finish or click to skip past video
3. Game crashes before reaching main menu

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @skulwicki, as a quick test, can you also check how the game behaves with the [bleeding edge](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Proton-Versions#proton-bleeding-edge) Proton Experimental build?

The bleeding edge beta branch appears to have everything working as expected. I was able reach the main menu and start a game as expected. Do you have any ideas what changed between current experimental and the bleeding edge that might have broken and then fixed this issue?

You can fix the video issue while using proton experimental, using ffmpeg and convert the mp4 files using the VP9 codec. 

Konsole:

ffmpeg -i Logo_Asobo.mp4 -c:v libvpx-vp9 -b:v 2M -c:a libopus output.mp4

Then rename the original e.g.  Logo_Asobo.mp4 to something else and put the output.mp4 with the name of Logo_Asobo.mp4 in the videos folder of MSFS2024.

This should be: SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/Limitless/Packages/fs-base-videos/Videos

The NVIDIA driver is broken in general when it comes to DX12 (usually games run but with a **massive** performance penalty) and completely broken when it comes to specific titles like MSFS (2020 & 2024).

See for example here:
https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/microsoft-flight-simulator-crashes-with-status-vkacquirenextimagekhr-while-loading-on-wayland-since-555/305443

HDR works under wayland by building proton-tkg bleeding edge with wayland support, unfortunately the mouse pointer is not working.

Beyond of scope so no help with this, but if anyone wants to try it, you will need
1) install vk_hdr_layer (google it)
2) clone wine-tkg-git, set `_wayland_driver="false" to "true"` on proton-tkg.cfg before building proton-tkg bleeding edge
3) add key HKCU\Software\Wine\Drivers\Graphics = x11,wayland (tip: `protontricks -c "wine regedit" 2537590`)
4) launch game with `DXVK_HDR=1 ENABLE_HDR_WSI=1 DISPLAY= %command% -FastLaunch`

following_discussion.discussion_body:
> The game installed fine and ran great (platinum) with d3dcompiler_47 and win7 until 22.09.22 where i can no longer join any public match

this is the update that broke it: https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/2087030/view/3395173894699741120
after that a friend and I could no longer join matches.

I helped the developer to fix the game and new update is scheduled to land within 24h hours to make the game work for all linux users. ^-^

The linux module for EOS is enabled since 23rd Aug 2022:
![shatterline-linux-eac-module](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11302521/192392346-6110b702-3272-40b7-a768-5bee3e0c1943.png)

The developer did setup according to the provided documentation in https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/steamdeck/proton#1 and noted the requirement to update the linux module.

There was a bug in the pipeline which caused the public release of the game to miss `Bin/CrashRpt1403.dll` which was triggering the EOS resulting in linux players being unable to join any public game lobby the solution is to provide the file. Discovered through searching in `Steam/steamapps/compatdata/2087030/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Saved Games/Shatterline/somerandomchars/user/log/Game.log` which included:

> <2022-09-26T22:09:54:789+02> [Warning] (Game) - [EOSCore_Windows::EOSSDKLoggingCallback] LogEOSAntiCheat: [AntiCheatClient] [PollStatusInternal] Client Violation with Type: 5 Message: Unknown file version (Bin/CrashRpt1403.dll), len: 44

The `d3dcompiler_47.dll` **was required** as they broke shaders for proton which will be fixed after the game realease and that currently results in a black textures when you try to join the game, with new update that dependency is provided by upstream so **it's no longer required to workaround it** and workarounds will trigger EOS.

The `win7` workaround is still needed as the game is using **Winsock Registered Input/Output (RIO) API** that is not implemented in wine/proton if the windows release is set on 8 and higher, developer plans to address it before the game gets released or shortly after by replacing it with proton-friendly alternative.

The game is made by former crytek developers who worked on the cryengine so for the game they are using lumberyard fork which currently doesn't support linux exports. The developer is working on porting the renderer and libraries to get steam deck certified and native linux version with it after the game's release.

Relevant discussion on:
* shatterline discord with the devs https://discord.com/channels/750704373113552976/1022511212661067857 
* with frogs https://discord.com/channels/853130811581530142/855613414584221706/1024017540789305414

Proposed long term solution for issues alike is to fix vkd3d-shader used in the d3dcompiler in wine to compile HLSL shaders without the need to use 3rd party d3dcompiler_47 and mentioned RIO API.

---

If you like the game then consider buying the 5 USD/EUR trinket in the game to support the development and work on linux port and proton compatibility

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e101fd76-cc28-4b5a-9769-55e88871e677)

The game won't let you into an online match unless it's able to verify the signatures of **ALL** game files which is likely your case (thus the suggestion in https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6165#issuecomment-2186014531) as either you or your system must have changed something for the anti-cheat to fail it's checks -> Verify the game integrity and it should work again.

Note that on Linux under proton the game has much stricter anti-cheat than on Windows as the Linux's design enables for a deeper integration with the OS and the kernel. following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @PorcelainMouse, looking more closely at your issue report, you're using Feral Interactive's native linux port of the game. Proton is a compatibility layer to run Windows based games on Linux, and since you're using the native linux variant of the game, this project isn't involved at all with your issue.

Thanks for looking at it! You know, that's what I thought, but I figured I didn't understand proton well enough to be sure.  I was just following Steam's support instructions.  I guess I'm dead in the water.  Their support wasn't able to fix it, either.

Don't know about desktop PCs, but on my notebook {5800H & RTX3070} DLSS is not working, I tried blacklist AMD iGPU, update nvapi and nvngx_dlss, insert '__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD=1 __GLX_VENDOR_LIBRARY_NAME=nvidia PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1  PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU=0 %command%'. Log always report:
`DLSS support test, code = 0x00000001, info: NVSDK_NGX_Result_Success`
`Disabling DLSS as it is not supported on non Nvidia cards`

I insert **DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/home/wer/.local/dxvk.conf DXVK_FILTER_DEVICE_NAME="NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop" PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 %command%** to game settings window.
 **dxvk.conf** contain only:
`dxgi.nvapiHack = False`
And voila DLSS working now.
Its strange, but DLSS don't provide additional performance, 100fps w/o any AA and 104 with DLSS quality, also GPU heavily underclocked, sometimes only 55-65% load.

`PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU=0 DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=(path-to-file)` with `dxgi.nvapiHack = False` is enough to get DLSS to work fine for me.
